current_state: The school operates with seven Schoolwide Learner Outcomes: Bilingual Communicators, Compassionate, Confident, Engaged, Innovative, Reflective, and Responsible. Only Confident carries a published behavioral descriptor. The other six function as single-word labels. Unit plans across AAO - Academic Affairs Office, Art Department, Business and Economics, College Counseling Department, Curriculum and Teaching, English, Future Scholar Innovation Center, Humanities, Library, Math, Music and Sports Department, Sciences, and SAO - Student Affairs Office (Pastoral and Well-Being) do not consistently name which SLOs each unit advances. The gradebook does not carry an SLO tag on assessment artifacts. PLC cycles run without an opening SLO frame or a closing SLO-tagged evidence entry. Term reports to Parents carry subject grades without an SLO-organized narrative section. Students do not use SLO vocabulary in self-reflection at term reporting or portfolio review. The Parent Committee, Parents, Students, Staff / Faculty, and Administration / Leadership do not share a common SLO vocabulary for what student growth means. Student data conversations rarely cite an SLO as the interpretive lens. The annual self-study against WASC standards is not organized with the seven SLOs as evidence categories.
desired_state: Each of the seven SLOs carries a school-authored behavioral descriptor at the depth of the current Confident descriptor, drafted by faculty teams drawing from Art Department, Business and Economics, English, Humanities, Math, Sciences, Music and Sports Department, and SAO - Student Affairs Office (Pastoral and Well-Being), validated by the Director of Teaching and Curriculum Center, the Director of AAO, the Director of Student Affairs Office, the Heads of Department, and the IHS Principal, and published in the staff handbook, the parent handbook, and student-facing materials by 2025-11-28. The unit-planning template and the gradebook are updated by the Director of Teaching and Curriculum Center with the Senior Teaching Coordinator and the PD Leader so that every department's Term 2 unit plans name one or two SLOs per unit and carry at least one SLO-tagged assessment artifact, with PLC cycles in Art Department, Business and Economics, College Counseling Department, English, Humanities, Math, Music and Sports Department, and Sciences opening on an SLO frame and closing on SLO-tagged evidence, reaching 90 percent of logged cycles in the 2025-2026 cycle. Term reporting carries an SLO-organized narrative section for every enrolled student in IG1, IG2, AS, and A2. Every student in IG1, IG2, AS, and A2 produces an SLO self-reflection at term reporting and at portfolio review. The annual self-study evidence binder for WASC A1, A4, B1, B2, B3, and C1 is compiled with the seven SLOs as the organizing evidence categories by 2026-07-24.
rationale: The school commits in its vision that students be engaged and realize their academic potential and become life-long learners fully capable of leveraging the science of how learning happens. The seven SLOs are the operational expression of Academic excellence, Caring and safe community, and Global citizenship. Six of the seven SLOs lack a shared behavioral descriptor, so faculty across Art Department, Business and Economics, English, Humanities, Math, Music and Sports Department, and Sciences cannot tag units or assessments against a common definition, and Students, Parents, the Parent Committee, Staff / Faculty, and Administration / Leadership do not share a common vocabulary for what growth means. WASC standard A1 requires the mission be collectively understood and implemented by the school community. WASC standard B1 requires curriculum that is standards aligned and responds to student needs. WASC standard B3 requires varied assessments that measure student progress against those standards. WASC standard C1 requires shared responsibility for student growth across the community. The plan addresses the Bilingual Environment, Curriculum and Learning, Faculty PD and PLC, School Culture, Student Data and Well-being, Community Involvement, and Resources and Tools areas for improvement by giving the seven SLOs descriptors, unit-plan placement, assessment tagging, PLC cycle framing, parent reporting structure, and self-study organizing categories during the 2025-2026 accreditation cycle.
student_impact_framing: Students in IG1, IG2, AS, and A2 encounter the seven SLOs as named learning targets inside each unit rather than as wall posters. Assessment artifacts carry SLO tags, so a student can see which SLOs a piece of their work demonstrates. At term reporting and at portfolio review, students write an SLO self-reflection naming which SLOs a given piece of their work demonstrates. Parents receive an SLO-organized narrative section in each term report alongside subject grades, so home conversations about progress can use the same SLO vocabulary the student and teacher use. PLC-driven adjustments to instruction are organized by SLO-tagged evidence, so instructional changes during the year are tied to named outcomes the student is working toward.
provenance: The plan draws on the existing published Confident descriptor as the depth benchmark for the other six SLO descriptors. It draws on the seven enumerated Schoolwide Learner Outcomes: Bilingual Communicators, Compassionate, Confident, Engaged, Innovative, Reflective, and Responsible. It draws on the three mission clauses GROUNDED, ROUNDED, and UNBOUNDED and the six vision clauses covering engagement, life-long learning, personal responsibility, self-esteem, bilingual communication in Chinese and English, and compassion. It draws on WASC accreditation standards A1, A4, B1, B2, B3, and C1 as the criteria the self-study evidence binder will address. It draws on the four year groups IG1, IG2, AS, and A2 as the population scope for unit-plan coverage, term reporting, and portfolio review. It draws on the seven areas for improvement: Bilingual Environment, Curriculum and Learning, Faculty PD and PLC, School Culture, Student Data and Well-being, Community Involvement, and Resources and Tools. It draws on the divisions enumerated in the school's organizational roster, in particular AAO - Academic Affairs Office, Curriculum and Teaching, SAO - Student Affairs Office (Pastoral and Well-Being), Art Department, Business and Economics, College Counseling Department, English, Humanities, Library, Math, Music and Sports Department, and Sciences. It draws on the stakeholder groups Administration / Leadership, Staff / Faculty, Students, Parents, and Parent Committee as the community that must share the SLO vocabulary.
